  "decision": "fail",

  "percent_complete": 100,

  "confidence": 1.0,

  "rate": 0.0,
  "order": [],
  "workable": [],
  "findings": [
    "Differential pressure impulse lines are sloped incorrectly for the service type.",
    "Gas taps are located below the pipe centerline, leading to liquid accumulation in the lines.",
    "Liquid taps are located on top of the pipe, leading to gas pocket entrapment in the lines."
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Isolate the transmitter and impulse lines from the process.",
    "Re-route and re-slope the impulse lines according to API RP 551 guidelines (gas taps on top, liquid taps on the side/bottom).",
    "Re-commission and calibrate the transmitter before returning it to service."
  ],
  "rationale": "The DP impulse lines are installed upside down relative to standard practice for gas and liquid services. This incorrect slope causes gas pockets to trap in liquid lines and liquid/condensate to accumulate in gas lines, leading to measurement errors, sluggish response, or complete loss of signal. Because the asset is in-service, this constitutes an active operational failure and non-compliance with API RP 551.",
  "references": [
    "API RP 551 (Process Measurement Instrumentation)",
    "Manufacturer Installation Manuals"
  ]
}
